state.current_state: Spanish pilot of the knightship La Niña de Córdoba, age implied ~23-27. Took viaticum at the Chapel of Havana after 5-year excommunication, lifted by an unknown bishop in exchange for joining Cortés's fleet. Has piloted his Reliquary mecha for 8 years; expects to die by 28 or 29. Killed his first Moor at age 23 outside Granada. Wrote his mother a second letter the afternoon before sailing. Sails for the Americas on the morning tide.

evidence_spans: Andrés knelt at the Chapel of Havana's altar, palms flat on the flagstones, forehead to the stone beside them.
He had been ten when the Order of Santiago took him, sixteen when he first put his hands on the controls, twenty-three when he killed his first Moor at the wall of a village outside Granada.
He was the pilot. He was the saint. He was the machine. He was the hum.

state.current_state: A second-class knightship/Reliquary mecha, fifty feet of gilded plate, monstrance enshrining the relics (finger, tooth, small rib) of the saint Flora. Stands in her cradle on the quay at Havana, awaiting morning tide. Emits a constant hum that grows when piloted; calls Andrés to her even at distance.

evidence_spans: stood in her cradle on the quay outside, fifty feet of gilded plate in the failing light. Second-class. A knightship, the captains called it. A Reliquary.
Her monstrance glowed soft, the soft light of something preserved in crystal, a finger, a tooth, a small rib, all that was left of a girl killed in 850
The vibration moved through the planks of the mole into the soles of his boots, into the bones of his wrists.

state.current_state: Saint of the Faith. Girl killed in 850 by men who asked her to recant and were refused. Her relics (finger, tooth, small rib) animate La Niña de Córdoba. Her name is unspoken aloud by the expedition.

evidence_spans: all that was left of a girl killed in 850 by men who had asked her to recant and been refused.
He did not speak the name. No one in the expedition did. There were saints of the Faith whose names you did not say aloud.

*Flora.*

*Child of Córdoba.*

*Little Flower.*

*Child-Martyr of the Mozarabes.*

state.current_state: Stone chapel on Havana harbor. Holds Seville-carved saints on its walls, two candles, a painted copy of Our Lady of Antigua donated by an unnamed sailor. Dead are interred beneath the flagstones. Site of Andrés's viaticum on the eve of departure.

evidence_spans: Andrés knelt at the Chapel of Havana's altar, palms flat on the flagstones, forehead to the stone beside them.
Two candles burned beside a painted copy of Our Lady of Antigua, donated by a sailor whose name he would never know.
He walked out past the Seville-carved saints on the chapel walls, into the dusk on the harbor mole.

state.current_state: Commander of the fleet at Havana that sails on the morning tide for the Americas. Andrés earned his berth in the fleet in exchange for the lifting of his excommunication.

evidence_spans: The letter from a bishop he had never met, lifting his excommunication in exchange for a berth on Cortés's fleet, was folded twice in his doublet.

state.current_state: Military-religious order that took Andrés at age ten and trained him as a Reliquary pilot. Forbids thoughts of Heaven, reward, conquest, or glory; demands focus only on the Faith and service of God and the Emperor.

evidence_spans: He had been ten when the Order of Santiago took him
The Order said it was wrong, to think of Heaven, to think of reward, to think of anything but the Faith and the service of God and the Emperor.

state.current_state: Another ship/vessel of the fleet at Havana. Its men watch Andrés walk the mole in silence.

evidence_spans: The men of the *Santa Bárbara* watched him pass, and they did not speak.

state.current_state: Slave girl in a Mayan compound for about a year. Speaks Nahuatl (taught by her father, 'before'), Mayan, and a little of the coast tongue. Cautious, watchful; survived a near-incursion by a drunk guard the prior night. Has agreed to befriend the boy Rabbit if anything happens to Citlal.

evidence_spans: Malintzin woke before the sun cleared the stockade.
"I speak Nahuatl and Mayan and a little of the coast tongue," she said. "I have not been asked for anything in a year that was not a taking. I will be his friend."
"My father. Before."

state.current_state: Older slave woman, Mayan-faced but with Mexica carriage. New to the kitchen-hut work in Malintzin's compound. Was to be married before her city did not survive the season; aunt to the boy Rabbit. Asks Malintzin to befriend Rabbit if anything happens to her, suggesting she anticipates her own removal.

evidence_spans: "I am Citlal."
"I was going to be married," Citlal said. "The marriage did not happen. The city did not survive the season in which it was to have happened. That is most of it."
"He is my sister's son."

state.current_state: Tall boy enslaved in the same compound, called Rabbit by the overseer because he runs. Works the field with the men. Has not been beaten this week. Citlal's sister's son; was to be married to Citlal's cousin (now lost). Once boasted that his aunt was a princess.

evidence_spans: "The boy in the field. The tall one, the one the overseer calls Rabbit because he runs."
"He is well. He works with the men. He has not been beaten this week."
"He said, once, that his aunt was a princess."

state.current_state: Tlatoani of the Mexica. Has slept four hours across two nights and not eaten. Received a runner reporting eleven pale-stranger ships at Tabasco and an astronomical report that Venus rose three fingers north of mark, signaling Quetzalcoatl. Has dispatched priests to Cholula and Tula to confirm engine-seals.

evidence_spans: Moctezuma sat cross-legged on his mat, turquoise diadem set straight on his brow but his face worn under it.
He ordered priests to Cholula. He ordered priests to Tula. He ordered the runner fed and held.
He looked as a man looks who has slept four hours across two nights.

state.current_state: Eldest astronomer of the narrator's calmecac. Reported Venus's irregular rising to Moctezuma as a sign of the Feathered Serpent. Has ordered the narrator to withhold a recurring dream from all ears, including his father's, until noon (nepantla tonatiuh), when he must tell the tlatoani.

evidence_spans: Quauhtli, eldest astronomer of my own calmecac, his turquoise ear-spool catching the brazier-light, stepped forward with Tlacaelle and two older students.
Quauhtli had heard the dream once and ordered me to keep it from every ear, including my father's, until the sun stood at nepantla tonatiuh.
"After noon," he said. "Then you will tell the tlatoani. Not before."

state.current_state: Priest of the calmecac who accompanied Quauhtli before the tlatoani at the dawn audience to deliver the Venus report.

evidence_spans: Quauhtli, eldest astronomer of my own calmecac, his turquoise ear-spool catching the brazier-light, stepped forward with Tlacaelle and two older students.

state.current_state: Sixteen-year-old scribe-priest of the calmecac, two years in training, has stood guard at six audiences. Held honor guard at eastern wall during dawn audience. Has dreamed three nights of an obsidian scrying pool in which his own face spoke a tongue not Nahuatl. Ordered by Quauhtli to disclose the dream to Moctezuma at noon.

evidence_spans: I was sixteen. I had been a scribe-priest of the calmecac for two years and had stood guard at six audiences, and I had never seen him like this.
I had dreamed it three nights running. An obsidian scrying pool. My own face in the black water, speaking.
I had been ordered to hold a dream. I held the thought with it.

state.current_state: Mexica engine humming beneath the audience floor of the precinct, with twin shrines of Huitzilopochtli and Tlaloc. Its hum did not change at the report of Venus's irregular rising — its silence on the matter is read as its own answer.

evidence_spans: The Great Engine hummed beneath the audience floor, a low constant liturgy in stone, the twin shrines of Huitzilopochtli and Tlaloc thrumming through my heels where I stood honor guard at the eastern wall.
Beneath the floor the Engine's hum did not change, and the not-changing of it was its own answer.

state.current_state: Site of a dormant Quetzalcoatl engine that has slept for generations. Moctezuma has dispatched priests there to confirm the seals after Venus walked early.

evidence_spans: The dormant Quetzalcoatl engines at Cholula and at Tula had slept for generations.
He ordered priests to Cholula. He ordered priests to Tula.

state.current_state: Site of a dormant Quetzalcoatl engine that has slept for generations. Priests dispatched by Moctezuma to confirm the seals.

evidence_spans: The dormant Quetzalcoatl engines at Cholula and at Tula had slept for generations.
He ordered priests to Cholula. He ordered priests to Tula.

state.current_state: River on the eastern coast (Chontal name) where eleven pale-stranger ships are anchored, reported by a dust-stained runner at Moctezuma's dawn audience.

evidence_spans: their houses-on-the-water anchored at the river the Chontal call Tabasco.
Eleven, the runner said.

state.current_state: Twin-shrined pyramid in the Mexica precinct, steps bone-white at morning hour. The narrator has a fleeting thought, while passing a corridor window, that 'the stones could be taken down.'

evidence_spans: The Templo Mayor stood against the morning, the steps bone-white at this hour, the twin shrines small at the summit against a sky still pink at its edges.
I felt that the stones could be taken down.

state.current_state: The Feathered Serpent, associated with Venus (the morning star). Venus's irregular rising — three fingers north of mark — is read as Quetzalcoatl's sign, not Moctezuma's. Linked to the dormant engines at Cholula and Tula.

evidence_spans: Tlahuizcalpantecuhtli was walking early. The sun, Quauhtli said carefully, rose as it should, Huitzilopochtli kept his order. But Venus did not. This was not the tlatoani's sign. This was the Feathered Serpent's.
The dormant Quetzalcoatl engines at Cholula and at Tula had slept for generations.
